name: Millet Loaf
recipeInstructions:
You can serve this versatile loaf for lunch or as an appetizer. Place slices on a bed of Bibb lettuce with vegetable garnishes and a light, creamy salad dressing.
1. Rinse the millet and place it in a medium saucepan with the water and 2 tsp sea salt. Cook the millet, covered, over medium heat for about 30 minutes or until soft; the millet should absorb all of the water.
2. If the grains are too moist, the loaf will not bind properly.
3. Sauté the carrots, celery, onions, and garlic in oil for 6 minutes, or until the onions are translucent.
4. Add the seasonings, including the remaining 2 tsp sea salt. Mix the cooked millet and vegetables together, along with the nuts or seeds, if desired.
5. Mix the two flours together and add them to the millet mixture, blending it well so the loaf will hold together.
6. Lightly oil and flour a large loaf pan. Press the millet mixture into the pan.
7. Bake in a preheated oven at 400°F for about one hour. If the millet mixture is warm when placed in the pan, reduce the baking time to about 45 minutes.
8. Allow the loaf to cool for 10 minutes, then carefully remove it from the pan. To avoid breaking the loaf, you may wish to slice it while it is still in the pan.
